en:
Analytical psychology has already been reproached with setting at liberty the animal instincts of men, hitherto happily repressed, and causing thereby untold harm. This childish apprehension clearly proves how little trust is put in the efficacy of present-day moral principles. It is pretended that only morals can restrain men from dissoluteness; a much more efficient regulator, however, is necessity , which sets much more real and convincing bounds than any moral principles. It is true that analysis liberates animal instincts, but not, as some have said, just in order to let them loose, but rather to make them available for higher application, in so far as this is possible to the particular individual, and in so far as such "sublimated" application is required. Under all circumstances it is an advantage to be in full possession of one's own personality, for otherwise the repressed desires will get in the way in a most serious manner, and overthrow us just in that place where we are most vulnerable. It is surely better that a man learn to tolerate himself, and instead of making war on himself convert his inner difficulties into real experiences, rather than uselessly repeat them again and again in phantasy. Then at least he lives, and does not merely consume himself in fruitless struggles. But when men are educated to recognise the baser side of their own natures, it may be hoped they will learn to understand and love their fellow-men better too. A decrease of hypocrisy and an increase of tolerance towards oneself, can have only good results in tolerance towards one's neighbours, for men are only too easily disposed to extend to others the unfairness and violence which they do to their own natures.

Freud's theory of repression does, indeed, seem to postulate the existence only of people who, being too moral, are continually repressing the immorality of their natural instincts. According to this idea, the immoral man who allows his natural instincts an unbridled existence should be proof against neurosis. But daily experience proves this is obviously not the case; he may be just as neurotic as other men. If we analyse him, we find that it is simply his decency that has been repressed. Therefore, when an immoral man is neurotic, he represents what Nietzsche appropriately described as "the pale criminal," a man who does not stand upon the same level as his deed. [232]

The opinion may be held, that in such a case the repressed remnants of decency are merely infantile traditional legacies, that impose unnecessary fetters upon natural instincts, for which reason they should be eradicated. The principle "écraser l'infâme" would be the natural culmination of such an absolute let-instinct-live theory. [233] That would obviously be quite phantastic and nonsensical. It should, indeed, never be forgotten—and the Freudian School needs this reminder—that morality was not brought down upon tables of stone from Sinai and forced upon the people, but that morality is a function of the human soul, which is as old as humanity itself. Morality is not inculcated from without. Man has it primarily within himself—not the law indeed, but the essence of morals.

After all, does a more moral view-point exist than the let-instinct-live theory? Is there a more heroic morality than this? That is why Nietzsche, the heroic, is especially partial to it. It is natural and inborn cowardice that makes people say, "God preserve me from following my instincts," thinking that they thus prove their high moral standard. They do not understand that following one's bent is really much too costly for them, too strenuous, too dangerous, and finally it cuts somewhat against that sense of decency which most people associate rather with taste than with a categorical imperative. The unpardonable fault of the let-instinct-live theory is, that it is much too heroic, too idealogic for the multitude.
zh:
分析心理學曾被指責釋放了人類迄今為止被幸福壓抑的動物本能，並因此造成了無窮的禍害。這種幼稚的憂慮清楚地證明，人們對當今道德原則的效力是多麼缺乏信任。人們假裝只有道德才能約束人不致放縱；然而，一個更有效的調節器是「必要性」，它設下的界限比任何道德原則都更真實、更具說服力。確實，分析釋放了動物本能，但並非如某些人所說，只是為了放縱它們，而是為了使它們能夠用於更高層次的應用——只要這對特定個人是可能的，並且只要這種「昇華」的應用是必要的。無論如何，完全擁有自己的個性總是一種優勢，否則被壓抑的慾望會以最嚴重的方式阻礙我們，並在我們最脆弱的地方將我們擊倒。一個人學會容忍自己，與其對自己開戰，不如將內心的困難轉化為真實的經驗，而不是在幻想中無益地一再重複它們，這無疑是更好的。這樣，他至少是在生活，而不只是在徒勞的鬥爭中消耗自己。但當人們被教育去認識自己本性中較低劣的一面時，可以希望他們也將學會更好地理解和愛他們的鄰舍。虛偽的減少和對自己的寬容增加，只會對寬容鄰舍產生良好的結果，因為人們太容易將他們對待自己本性的不公和暴力，也施加在他人身上。

佛洛伊德的壓抑理論，確實似乎假定只存在那些過於道德、不斷壓抑其自然本能之不道德性的人。根據這個觀點，那個讓其自然本能不受約束地存在的不道德之人，應該對神經症免疫。但日常經驗證明這顯然並非如此；他可能和其他人一樣是神經症患者。如果我們分析他，我們會發現，恰恰是他的「正直」被壓抑了。因此，當一個不道德之人患有神經症時，他所代表的是尼采恰當地描述為「蒼白的罪犯」的那種人，一個其行為與其自身層次不相稱的人。[232]

或許有人會認為，在這種情況下，被壓抑的正直殘餘不過是幼稚的傳統遺產，它們對自然本能施加了不必要的束縛，因此應該被根除。「消滅醜惡」[^233] 的原則將是這種絕對的「順從本能」理論的自然頂點。這顯然是相當荒謬和無稽的。確實，永遠不應忘記——而佛洛伊德學派正需要這個提醒——道德並非從西奈山的石版上降下並強加給人們，而是人類靈魂的一種功能，它與人類本身一樣古老。道德並非從外部灌輸。人最初就在自身之內擁有它——確實不是律法，而是道德的本質。

畢竟，是否存在比「順從本能」理論更道德的觀點？有比這更英雄式的道德嗎？這就是為什麼英雄式的尼采特別偏愛它的原因。正是天生的怯懦使人們說：「願上帝保佑我不跟隨我的本能」，以為這樣就證明瞭他們高尚的道德標準。他們不明白，順從自己的傾向對他們來說實在是代價太高、太費力、太危險，最終還有些違背那種大多數人與其說是與絕對命令、不如說是與品味相關聯的正直感。「順從本能」理論不可饒恕的過錯在於，它對大眾來說實在是太英雄式、太理想主義了。

[^233]: 原文為法文「écraser l'infâme」，意為「消滅醜惡」，是伏爾泰的口號。
